feed.title: DOSACC Accounting Industry Intelligence Feed
feed.description: Curated, executive-level developments for CPA and accounting firm leaders across the United States and Australia. Headlines link to original publishers; summaries, 'why it matters' notes and DOSACC insights are produced by the DOSACC research desk and are not authored or endorsed by the publishers.
feed.audience: CPA firm owners, partners, directors, audit managers and tax managers

articles.headline: PCAOB's QC 1000 and AS 2901 Take Effect December 15, 2026
articles.summary: New PCAOB quality control standard QC 1000 and AS 2901 become effective December 15, 2026. QC 1000 requires firms to build risk-based quality control systems that proactively identify and manage threats to audit quality, shifting accountability upstream rather than relying on post-issuance inspection findings to surface deficiencies.
articles.whyItMatters: Every firm auditing SEC issuers must document a formal, risk-based quality management system before year-end. Smaller firms face the heaviest relative compliance lift, and gaps will be visible in 2027 inspections.
articles.dosaccInsight: DOSACC can staff the recurring monitoring, testing and documentation cycles QC 1000 demands, freeing senior auditors to own risk assessment and remediation rather than evidence administration.

articles.headline: PCAOB Forms Inspections Modernization Council to Overhaul Its Inspection Program
articles.summary: On May 28, 2026, the PCAOB launched an Inspections Modernization Council, an advisory body bringing outside expertise into reshaping how inspections operate. The Board is seeking feedback on aligning inspections with its new quality control regime, the information most useful to stakeholders, and how inspection reporting could be enhanced.
articles.whyItMatters: Inspection methodology drives where firms invest review effort. A quality-control-focused inspection model rewards firms with mature, documented systems and penalizes ad hoc, file-by-file remediation.
articles.dosaccInsight: Firms should treat the transition window as an opportunity to standardize workpapers. DOSACC delivery teams can apply a consistent documentation template across engagements so inspection readiness is built in, not bolted on.

articles.headline: Big Four Race to Scale Agentic AI Across Audit and Advisory
articles.summary: EY has moved roughly 1,000 AI agents into development or production with a target of 100,000 by 2028, investing over $1 billion annually. PwC's agent OS now runs about 25,000 agents; KPMG's Workbench connects dozens of agents alongside Clara, its audit platform used by more than 95,000 auditors globally.
articles.whyItMatters: Agentic AI is moving from pilots to production at the largest firms, resetting client expectations on turnaround and price. Mid-market firms that do not respond risk competing on yesterday's cost structure.
articles.dosaccInsight: DOSACC pairs trained offshore professionals with AI-assisted workflows, giving smaller firms enterprise-grade throughput without the capital outlay of building an agentic platform in-house.

articles.headline: KPMG to Cut U.S. Audit Partner Ranks by 10% for 2026
articles.summary: KPMG is reducing its U.S. audit partner ranks by roughly 10% for 2026 to address a senior-level bottleneck created by low attrition and AI absorbing routine audit work. The move mirrors broader Big Four restructuring, with thousands of roles cut across UK and global operations over three years.
articles.whyItMatters: AI is compressing the traditional audit pyramid from the bottom and the top. Firms must rethink leverage models, promotion timelines and how junior talent gains the experience that automation now performs.
articles.dosaccInsight: As large firms thin their leverage, mid-market firms can absorb displaced demand. DOSACC provides flexible offshore capacity to scale into that opportunity without committing to fixed senior headcount.

articles.headline: AICPA Launches Profession Ready Initiative to Future-Proof CPA Talent
articles.summary: In February 2026 the AICPA unveiled its Profession Ready Initiative, a roadmap to prepare the next generation of CPAs as AI fundamentally changes the nature of work. A research phase of surveys runs throughout 2026, examining junior-hire volumes, required skill sets and how young professionals learn and advance.
articles.whyItMatters: The skills mix for entry-level staff is shifting from manual execution to analysis and judgment. Firms that wait for the AICPA's findings before adapting training risk a capability gap among new hires.
articles.dosaccInsight: DOSACC handles the high-volume, rules-based preparation work, letting firms redeploy their limited domestic juniors into the analytical, client-facing roles the AICPA identifies as the profession's future.

articles.headline: Accounting Graduate Pipeline Stays Thin as Retirements Accelerate
articles.summary: The AICPA & CIMA 2025 Trends Report shows declining CPA exam participation across several regions alongside rising retirements among experienced professionals. AI now supports or replaces many tasks once handled by junior staff, raising questions about hiring volumes even as the overall pipeline of qualified domestic talent contracts.
articles.whyItMatters: Fewer graduates plus accelerating partner retirements means a widening capacity gap precisely as demand for advisory work grows. Capacity, not just cost, is now the binding constraint for most firms.
articles.dosaccInsight: Offshore delivery is now a structural answer to a structural shortage, not a stopgap. DOSACC gives firms a dependable, scalable talent base while the domestic pipeline recovers.

articles.headline: More Than Half of Firms Now Outsource Part of Their Work
articles.summary: Industry analyses citing the AICPA MAP Survey indicate over 55% of firms now outsource some portion of their work, with adoption highest among mid-sized and Top 500 firms. Offshore hubs offer deep talent pools trained in U.S. standards, and firms report labor-cost reductions of 50% to 70% versus local hires.
articles.whyItMatters: Outsourcing has crossed from competitive edge to mainstream operating model. Firms still resisting it are increasingly out-priced and out-paced on capacity by peers who have already built offshore teams.
articles.dosaccInsight: The winning model blends offshore production for capacity and cost, automation for routine processes, and well-paid domestic staff for advisory work. DOSACC is purpose-built to deliver the first pillar.

articles.headline: 2026 Filing Season Brings Schedule 1-A, Form 1099-DA and OBBB Changes
articles.summary: The 2026 filing season implements the One Big Beautiful Bill: new Schedule 1-A for tip and overtime exclusions, Form 1099-DA for digital-asset reporting, a senior deduction of up to $6,000 per person, and a phase-out of paper refund checks in favor of electronic funds transfer.
articles.whyItMatters: Multiple new forms and deductions arrive at once, raising preparer review burden and error risk during peak season. Client communication on tips, overtime and crypto reporting becomes essential.
articles.dosaccInsight: DOSACC tax preparers absorb the added data-entry and reconciliation load from the new schedules and 1099-DA flows, so domestic reviewers can focus on planning conversations and final sign-off.

articles.headline: ASIC Sets FY2026-27 Financial Reporting, Audit and Sustainability Focus Areas
articles.summary: ASIC's release 26-098MR sets FY2026-27 focus areas, with plans to review 25 audit files spanning listed, unlisted and managed investment scheme entities. Reviews will target areas of significant judgment including revenue recognition, asset impairment, and the recognition and measurement of financial instruments.
articles.whyItMatters: ASIC is signalling exactly where it will probe. Australian audit teams and the firms supporting them should document judgment areas to a defensible standard before file selection.
articles.dosaccInsight: DOSACC can build standardized judgment-area workpaper templates for impairment, revenue and financial instruments, giving Australian firms consistent, inspection-ready documentation across the portfolio.

articles.headline: ASIC Escalates Enforcement on Late Financial Reports
articles.summary: ASIC has issued and collected on 21 infringement notices worth more than $4 million for alleged FY24 financial reporting breaches, including $792,000 across four Australian Canva Group entities for late lodgement. ASIC is using ATO data to identify companies that have outgrown reporting exemptions and fallen into breach.
articles.whyItMatters: Non-lodgement and auditor misconduct are now active enforcement priorities, with real financial penalties. Growing private companies that assumed they were exempt are squarely in scope.
articles.dosaccInsight: DOSACC can run a lodgement-status and exemption-threshold check across a firm's client base, flagging companies that have crossed reporting thresholds before ASIC's ATO-driven matching does.

articles.headline: Australia's Mandatory Climate Reporting Expands to Group 2 from July 2026
articles.summary: Mandatory climate disclosure under AASB S1/S2 (ASRS) commenced for Group 1 entities from January 2025 and extends to Group 2 entities for years beginning on or after 1 July 2026. Group 1 entities face limited assurance from July 2026, escalating to reasonable assurance from July 2028, with Scope 3 emissions phased in.
articles.whyItMatters: A large new cohort of reporters enters the regime mid-2026, and assurance is now mandatory. Firms need sustainability assurance capability and Scope 3 data expertise that few currently hold in-house.
articles.dosaccInsight: Sustainability assurance is a high-margin service line firms can launch now. DOSACC can support emissions data gathering, Scope 3 mapping and assurance evidence preparation as firms build the practice.

articles.headline: ISSA 5000 Sustainability Assurance Standard Effective December 15, 2026
articles.summary: ISSA 5000, the first comprehensive global standard for sustainability assurance, is effective for periods beginning on or after December 15, 2026, replacing ISAE 3000 (Revised). It applies to limited and reasonable assurance engagements across frameworks, with ISAE 3410 retained for separate greenhouse-gas conclusions.
articles.whyItMatters: Sustainability assurance is becoming a global, standardized profession with audit-grade rigor. Firms entering the space now can capture a fast-growing service line before it commoditizes.
articles.dosaccInsight: DOSACC can scale the evidence-gathering and testing labor behind ISSA 5000 engagements, letting firms offer sustainability assurance profitably without diverting scarce audit seniors from financial-statement work.

articles.headline: Client Advisory Services Post Double-Digit Growth and Rising Per-Head Fees
articles.summary: The CPA.com/AICPA PCPS CAS Benchmark Survey shows participating client advisory practices growing at a median 17%, with median net client fees per professional rising to $156,250, a 29% increase from 2022. Advisory-led, value-based and subscription pricing models are reshaping firm economics toward predictable, higher-margin revenue.
articles.whyItMatters: CAS is now the clearest path to margin expansion and recurring revenue. Firms anchored to hourly compliance billing are leaving both growth and profitability on the table.
articles.dosaccInsight: Advisory growth depends on freeing partner time from compliance. DOSACC takes over recurring bookkeeping, close and compliance work so firm leaders can build and sell higher-value advisory engagements.

articles.headline: Cybersecurity Threats Intensify as Firms Face GLBA and FTC Safeguards Scrutiny
articles.summary: Accounting firms head into 2026 facing escalating AI-powered threats, including deepfakes, automated phishing and credential theft, alongside ransomware and cloud misconfiguration risk. As entities subject to the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act and the FTC Safeguards Rule, firms must maintain layered, documented and continuously monitored security controls.
articles.whyItMatters: Client tax and financial data make firms prime targets, and regulators now expect documented programs, not best efforts. A breach carries client-loss, liability and regulatory consequences.
articles.dosaccInsight: DOSACC operates within documented access controls, MFA and monitored environments aligned to FTC Safeguards expectations, so outsourcing strengthens rather than dilutes a firm's control posture.

articles.headline: Private Equity Roll-Ups Reshape the Accounting Firm Landscape
articles.summary: IFAC reports over 1,000 accounting firms globally have seen private equity investment in the past decade. PE-backed accounting transactions rose from 22 in 2023 to 65 in 2024 and 104 in 2025, with January 2026 alone producing 25-plus deals, pushing firms toward EBITDA discipline and platform consolidation.
articles.whyItMatters: Capital is flooding the profession, raising competitive intensity and valuations. Firms must decide whether to consolidate, sell, or differentiate, and PE-backed platforms will compete aggressively on technology and scale.
articles.dosaccInsight: PE buyers prize clean, normalized EBITDA and scalable delivery. Standardizing production through DOSACC improves margins and operating discipline, strengthening a firm's position whether it sells, buys, or stays independent.

articles.headline: AICPA ENGAGE: Seven Principles to Improve CPA Firm Profitability
articles.summary: At AICPA ENGAGE in June 2026, practitioners outlined seven principles to improve firm profitability, spanning pricing discipline, right-sizing the client base, productivity through technology, and reallocating senior talent toward advisory work. The themes reinforce a shift from volume-based compliance toward value-based, margin-focused practice management.
articles.whyItMatters: Profitability is now a deliberate design choice. Firms that prune low-value clients, price for value and automate routine work materially outperform peers running on legacy realization habits.
articles.dosaccInsight: Right-sizing and re-pricing only work if capacity exists to serve retained clients well. DOSACC supplies that capacity, so firms can shed low-margin work without service gaps during the transition.

articles.headline: ATO and ASIC Tighten Data Sharing to Catch Reporting and Tax Gaps
articles.summary: ASIC is drawing on Australian Taxation Office data to identify companies that have outgrown reporting exemptions yet failed to lodge, signalling deeper inter-agency data matching. Combined with the ATO's continued compliance focus, growing private companies face higher odds of detection across both financial reporting and tax obligations.
articles.whyItMatters: Cross-agency data matching closes the gaps companies once slipped through. Advisers must proactively reassess clients' lodgement and tax positions as thresholds are crossed, rather than waiting for a notice.
articles.dosaccInsight: DOSACC can maintain a live obligations register per client, tracking turnover, size and lodgement status so Australian firms surface compliance triggers before agency data matching flags them.

articles.headline: PCAOB Seeks Feedback on Five-Year Strategic Plan Amid Leadership Shift
articles.summary: The PCAOB has requested public feedback on a five-year strategic plan amid leadership changes and a broader regulatory recalibration. The consultation covers oversight priorities, standard-setting direction and how the Board operates in an environment of complex technology ecosystems and new quality management standards.
articles.whyItMatters: The Board's strategic direction shapes years of standard-setting and inspection intensity. Firms and their advocates have a near-term window to influence priorities that will define audit obligations through 2030.
articles.dosaccInsight: Regulatory direction is trending toward systems and technology oversight. Firms that standardize delivery now, including through DOSACC, will adapt to whatever the strategic plan mandates with less disruption.
